A survey for a   certain year   found that   90 % of   pregnant   women received   medical  care   at   l east once before giving birth .   Of these women, 60 % received   medical   care  from   doctors, while   4 0 % received   medical   care from other health care   providers.  Given this   information , which   one   of the following statements can be inferred   with  certainty ?

options.A: More than half   of   the   pregnant   women   received   medical   care at least once from a  doctor.
options.B: Less   than half of   the   pregnant   women   received   medical   care at least once from a  doctor.
options.C: More than half of   the   pregnant   women   received   medical   care at   mo st once from a  doctor.
options.D: Less than half of   the   pregnant   women   received   medical   care at   most   once from a  doctor.

text: The country of Zombieland is in distress since more than 75% of its working  population is suffering from serious health issues. Studies conducted by competent  health experts concluded that a complete lack of physical exercise among its  working population w as one of the leading causes of their health issues. As one of  the measures to address the problem, the Government of Zombieland has decided  to provide monetary incentives to those who ride bicycles to work.  Based only on the information provided   above ,   which one of the following  statements can be   logically   inferred   with   certainty ?

options.A: All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work.
options.B: Riding bicycle s   will ensure that all of the working population of Zombieland is free  of health issues.
options.C: The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding  bicycles as mandatory.
options.D:
The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycl es is a form of physical  exercise.

text: GATE 2023 Computer Science and Information Technology (CS)  Let   f   :   A   →   B   be an onto (or surjective) function, where   A   and   B   are nonempty sets. Define an equivalence relation   ∼   on the set   A   as  a 1   ∼   a 2   if   f   ( a 1 ) =   f   ( a 2 )   ,  where   a 1 , a 2   ∈   A . Let   E   =   { [ x ] :   x   ∈   A }   be the set of all the equivalence classes under   ∼ .
